mainEntity.name: What is the turnover limit for mandatory GST audit?

acceptedAnswer.text: GST audit (GSTR-9C reconciliation statement) is mandatory for taxpayers with aggregate turnover exceeding Rs 5 crores in a financial year. This applies per GSTIN — multi-state businesses must check each registration separately.

mainEntity.name: Can the same CA do both statutory audit and GST audit?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. There is no restriction under GST law against the statutory auditor also certifying GSTR-9C. However, some companies prefer independent GST audit for additional assurance. Virtual Auditor can serve in either capacity.

mainEntity.name: What documents are needed for GST audit?

acceptedAnswer.text: Audited financial statements, trial balance with GST-wise breakup, all GST returns (GSTR-1, 3B, 2A/2B), e-way bill records, credit/debit notes, and RCM payment details. We provide a comprehensive checklist at engagement start.
